title: Airsquid

The '''airsquid''' was a type of floating [[Squid/Legends|squid]] that lived in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. Possessing a gasbag body, two [[Limb/Legends|wings]], and eighteen adhesive [[Tongue/Legends|tongues]], airsquids commonly attached themselves to the large, floating [[Beldon/Legends|beldon]] creatures in order to reach [[Cloud/Legends|clouds]] of airborne [[Algae/Legends|algae]] on which to [[Diet/Legends|feed]]. Airsquids could also form swarms to prey on larger creatures. At some point, a population of airsquids overran Bespin's abandoned [[Tibannopolis]] floating settlement.

==Biology and appearance==
The airsquid was a [[Species/Legends|species]] of small [[Squid/Legends|squid]] that resided in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]]–type [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sector]]. It had a buoyant, [[Color/Legends|black]] gasbag body, a pair of short [[Limb/Legends|wings]], and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] with sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] and a web of eighteen ribbon-like, adhesive [[Tongue/Legends|tongues]]. Airsquids possessed a measure of resilience to [[Telepathy/Legends|mind-influencing]] effects.<ref name="GATORW" />

==Behavior==
Airsquids frequently attached themselves to [[Beldon/Legends|beldons]],<ref name="GATORW" /> floating, spherical-gasbag herd creatures measuring seven [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in diameter on average that [[Diet/Legends|fed]] on airborne [[Algae/Legends|algae]].<ref name="GG2-2">[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin'', Second Edition]]</ref> Resembling groups of [[Barnacle/Legends|barnacles]] in that state, airsquids used the beldons as transportation in order to reach [[Cloud/Legends|clouds]] of algae, which they consumed by trapping them in their webs of tongues. Hunger could also drive<ref name="GATORW" /> the predatory<ref name="CSWE" /> airsquids to form swarms in order to overwhelm larger prey.<ref name="GATORW" />

==History==
[[File:Map_of_Tibannopolis.png|thumb|right|250px|Tibannopolis was home to a colony of airsquids.]]
At some point after the abandonment of the [[Tibannopolis]] floating platform following the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]]<ref name="GATORW" /> of [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> a colony of hundreds of airsquids overran the settlement. The creatures established at least seven nests there, including in the former business and residential districts.<ref name="GATORW" />

{{RPGseed}}
The [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of Bespin's [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] settlement at one point hired a group of individuals to eliminate the base on Tibannopolis that the Cloud City–based [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sawthawne]] was using to launch raids against the planet's [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] [[Gas/Legends|gas]] [[Refinery/Legends|refineries]]. To that end, the group attempted to sabotage Tibannopolis's [[Repulsorlift generator/Legends|repulsorlift generators]] in order to send the platform toppling down into Bespin's atmosphere; however, the local airsquid population posed a challenge to their efforts.<ref name="GATORW" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The airsquid was first mentioned in the [[2004]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''. The book contains a [[roleplaying game]] adventure seed involving airsquids that this article assumes plays out as described.<ref name="GATORW" />
